contract.inputs: contiguous F32, FP16, or BF16 [rows, hidden_size] plus matching [hidden_size] weight
contract.output: FP8 E4M3FN with the input logical shape
contract.scales: F32 [rows, 1] where normalized_weighted_value is approximately fp8_value * scale
contract.quantization: symmetric dynamic per-token with qmax 448 and minimum scale 1 / (448 * 512)
contract.rounding: input-scalar arithmetic boundaries followed by finite-saturating FP8 conversion
contract.stream: caller-selected CUDA stream; PyTorch adapter uses the current stream
contract.allocation: safe Rust and PyTorch out variants use caller-owned output and scale buffers

implementation.description: RMS reduction, normalized weighted absmax reduction, then FP8 quantization
implementation.small_shape_behavior: block size follows min(hidden_size, 1024) and CUB reductions receive the valid thread count
implementation.vectorization: four input elements and four FP8 output bytes per pack when alignment permits; scalar fallback otherwise

validation.torch_compile: dynamic FP8 unchecked out operator passed fullgraph compilation with bitwise vLLM output

raw_cuda.protocol: Cargo bench, CUDA events, CPU oracle before timing; no GEMM clock warmup

vllm_named_baseline.protocol: same preallocated PyTorch C++ custom-operator boundary, 1 second BF16 GEMM warmup per provider, 100 operator warmups, 15 samples, 2000 launches per sample
vllm_named_baseline.correctness: Loom output bytes and scales were bitwise identical to vLLM in both runs
vllm_named_baseline.interpretation: The operator-level advantage is order-stable: 47.2-51.6% by eager-dispatch ratio and 5.7-7.6% under CUDA Graph replay. This does not establish model-level benefit.

claim: Loom implements a vLLM-compatible fused RMSNorm plus dynamic per-token FP8 operator across Rust, CUDA, C ABI, and PyTorch. H20 evidence closes contract, correctness, named-baseline, stream, and CUDA Graph gates; a real engine workload gate remains open.
